mcp servers for cms this changes everything i think even more than the idea of cms having agents inside their interface having a mcp server that is easy to work with makes a cms super useful for instance if i can model content easily from the mcp now when i m creating a website or app that needs a new model or an update to a model i can just get my coding agent to take care of it for me don t forget to add this field to the model and update my typescript interfaces similarly if a user wants to do something like a find replace with langauge that doesn t fit within an organizations language guide sometimes those guide files are really huge they can just upload the file search their cms content for stuff that doesn t match and replace it mcp servers allow the user to be in control and to do whatever it is that they need to do i think it changes the cms landscape completely yes i ve been using the directus cms for few years and it s latest version now supports mcp this is absolutely a fantastic feature to have wait is there an agent for let s say wordpress to implement in backend yep taking your data to where people are so they can have multi platform ai interactions custom mcp is way more useful than a chat bot trapped inside crm i don t think we need chat interfaces for everything i have been pondering this question for a bit now is the concept of a website even needed anymore you could upload all of your marketing materials sales info support document etc into the mcp then have a very simple homepage where the user asks it questions or what they are looking for and ai dynamically creates a page with completely customized information followed with prompted questions like do you want to hear about this good point when you create an mcp server it should be more than just a wrapper around the api most api s are super generic and sometimes need very specific inputs the additional work of creating an mcp server and doing all the work to document the schemas for ai makes it much more likely to get good results why would a cms need a mcp server when you can simply connect with other ai api endpoints mcp servers asking for r empowerd check this out i am proud what we are doing in kontent ai i actually saw a demo of this in montreal from martin your vp of product i m cto of agility cms so i talked about it with him very exciting time for cms

do u have opinion on if parent node should handle all the code or if code should be broken up start with the parent suffer and split to smaller scripts suffer again and create a hybrid multilayer solution this is generally my heuristic it s too easy to get into the weeds engineering things just make stuff work and when it gets large delegate away the stuff that makes sense giving a script to every node is overkill giving every action to one script is overkill the balance is somewhere in the middle but only experimentation finds it usually i try to break it up because i hate parsing super long scripts but if the nodes interact a lot and i don t anticipate this ending up as a 2000 line monster i might put it all in the parent depends on what your doing why refactor all the time when you can do it right from the start small modular scrips with signals are almost always the best approach it prevents code repetition and your project gets more future and bug proof this is the whole philosophy behind godot s node system when would u use one over the other what defines a good object oriented design pattern and when to use which one is actually quite a large subject and it s hard to give a good answer because the answer is it depends some things i think about when deciding siblings should probably not communicate with each other so if i m tempted to write code where siblings are coupled i likely need to use a different pattern one where a parent node orchestrates operations between siblings children should probably not be manipulating their parents this will make maintenance later a nightmare because then who knows what is changing what so if i m writing code where a child modifies something in it s parent i need to use a different pattern of course these are just godot specific i still try to apply normal oop rules where i can such as encapsulation as an example if i have some parent that has an animated child the child should probably handle animating itself and expose an interface where the parent can manipulate the animation only in a valid way say a character holding a bow the character doesn t care about how the bow is animated just about when it s animation plays so the bow should provide its parent the character an interface to invoke the animation behavior and be notified when it s done without requiring the character to know anything about how the animation works however each scene works though in my view it is usually the case that the root level of some tree or subtree ought to be providing the external api to that tree or subtree that is to say scenes ought to be as small and reusable as possible while still doing their job therefore it seems to me that the root level node in a resource will always have some script attached however this is not really a design philosophy it is more of an empirical pattern that seems to arise out of general oop principles applied to godot there are limits and where you draw that line is largely personal preference if you have a button with a label child that displays extra text on top and you need to set the text from logic in the button are you really going to make a new script to set the text or would you just call label text text every node handling their own logic is not the point of oop and composition drawing the line at each node is completely arbitrary nodes can be extremely simple or extremely complex that is how the engine classes differentiate their functionality the chance that the game objects your game needs maps 1 1 onto the functionality of engine objects is not likely saying this needs it s own script because it s its own node has nothing to do with the design of your program the goal is to isolate the functionality you need into a single object even if it includes e g a couple of label nodes an animation player etc i almost exclusively use a pattern where children manages their parent and it s working better than any other approach for me for instance i have a death respawn component that checks the position of the parent and respawns it elsewhere if it goes under the killzone map holes during development i have another component that i can inject to a node in order to allow the node to be moved with the controller keyboard i can do that in debug and control anything for testing etc some of them components are state machines managing animations and input is a common recurring theme here its a nice pattern in godot because in godot you cannot mixin subclasses so as soon as you extend the node using its correct type say static body then you cannot add it to a characterbody or any other kind of node that doesn t subclass staticbody this means that you end up writing a staticbody script and a characterbody script that do the same thing annoying glue code fixed with the component pattern these are added to the nodes at runtime programmatically the advantage of doing it programmatically is that i can build assets scenes for their graphics animations collisions and let the code inject and manage their behavior this means my graphic artist doesn t have to think about linking any custom script which becomes the job of the programmer generally each node handling it s own logic is best doing everything in 1 script kinda defeats the point of godot an engine designed primarily for composition

lt gc gt turns into lt g gt if i wait a second neovim waits a specific amount of time h timeoutlen default 1 second after pressing any key while typing a mapping if no new key is pressed during that time neovim thinks that user finished typing a mapping and executes already pressed key one of the reasons is to account for conflicting mappings i e when one of the mappings is a prefix of another like in case of gc and gcc which are different mappings for historical reasons the comment line is a dedicated gcc mapping when you type only gc and wait neovim executes gc mapping and stops waiting for possible future gcc in turn gc is an operator which means that it waits for the user to type textobject or motion like aw ip etc operators in vim neovim are implemented through the h g built in keys which is why you see it appear on screen it means that neovim is waiting for you to type a textobject or motion try to comment current line or ip to comment current paragraph hope this helps help pages for timeoutlen in options txt g in map txt h help amp nbsp lt query gt amp nbsp about mistake donate reply amp nbsp rescan amp nbsp to amp nbsp check amp nbsp the amp nbsp comment amp nbsp again reply amp nbsp stop amp nbsp to amp nbsp stop amp nbsp getting amp nbsp replies amp nbsp to amp nbsp your amp nbsp comments hey thanks for the reply and it made me understand stuff clearly i ve read the defaults lua and comment lua to see how it works i just left it alone and added a keybind for lt c gt that invokes gcc either in insert or normal mode

switching to m series hello fellows i m currently using 2017 macbook pro 13 16 gb 256 gb it becomes quite sluggish at times mostly when i m on zoom or video calls and trying to do a task it takes time and start to heat up as well therefore i m considering upgrading to a m chip series but not sure which one i should go with i can afford m2 pro and may be m3 as well but which would be better overall in terms of speed performance etc buy what you can afford the newer without sacrificing what you actually need like storage or ram the better for longevity if you re coming from a 2017 machine the speed increase is massive and you wouldn t even be able to make the machines work so hard that you d reach the point where the difference between the m2 or m3 matters talking only about speed assuming that you d do what you usually do on your 2017 pro just buy an air you might not even blip the processors hard enough and never make the fans in a pro turn on and you basically just forked over more money for more ports and a better screen and a useless fan any apple silicon macbook will be a vast improvement if your heaviest usage is video calls you really don t need anything more powerful than an m1 macbook air power isn t the only thing to consider though some models have nicer screens for watching movies some models support more external monitors some models have more ports etc so maybe tell us about your needs in there areas and we might be able to help you narrow things down macbook pro with m2 pro is generally more powerful for demanding tasks like 3d work or heavy multi threaded workloads while the standard m3 macbook pro offers a solid more energy efficient option for lighter professional or everyday tasks

rid15 wasn t a bad show it just wasn t a good prime sequel i can never hate it because the figures were awesome and the designs more so but not calling it perfect i remember when i saw they were doing a figure of bisk and thought oh oh you are bringing creativity to help table that s true and i think the big problem is that it came at an odd time in the life of the franchise in the leadup to 2015 hasbro began getting rid of the one size fits all approach to the brand and started targeting different things at different audiences prime was designed as a flagship show for the hub network and aimed to carry over some of the older audience from the movies while rescue bots popped up to appeal to younger kids the toys also started becoming more and more separated with generations shifting to target the older teen adult market that was playing the video games and reading the comics while the toyline for prime got simpler and simpler and by the end of beast hunters was selling upscaled cyberverse commanders as voyagers in 2015 they also changed the age rating on the toys rather than being almost universally for ages 5 generations figures were for ages 8 while rid2015 toys were for ages 6 and rescue bots stuff continued being for 3 the intent seems to have been for transformers to have a tiered age system where fans would grow out of one segment of the brand and move on to something else so the kids who watched prime were seemingly expected to just move on to the new movies while rid was actually made for the kids who had been watching rescue bots and were now a little older it s definitely a bizarre decision to make a direct sequel to something that s not for the original show s audience but it s not like transformers hasn t done something like that before beast machines and i think now that we re a decade out the kids who were the right age for rid are starting to come out of the woodwork and it might have a better reputation in the future its certainly better as its own thing compared to a prime sequel but as its own thing its kinda not great still arguably without the connections to prime i think the show would be far worse the starscream mini arc and the soundwave episode in season 2 were pretty decent for what they were but solely because the show finally decided to commit to being a proper sequel to the show before i know the show undermines a lot of what came before so no argument to be had that it s a bad prime sequel but being a bad prime sequel probably still trumps over being a forgettable bad show no no it s also a bad show as a person who defends rid15 and thinks tfp was kind of a bad show rid15 also just fails on its own merits as well it was also pretty bad yeah if it was a stand alone show it probably would have been received better no it was a bad show on its own and even worse sequel i agree it s a perfectly fine kids show that i wouldn t have a problem with in the same vein as rescue bots but since it s a direct sequel to prime i have issues mainly the characterization of returning characters not mentioning previous characters and the trope of everything our heroes worked for meant nothing thing also i just don t like grimlock i agree

rustdesk fails to connect yes it does likewise when i ping the ip address it pings just fine thanks if you ping the domain name of your rustdesk server does it return the correct ip their support is very good i ve had a couple minor issues along the way they they ve helped with thanks very much for the reply risaw1981 yes the clients i am usually trying to connect to are on the same network as the host i am trying to connect from outside the network i will check out nat loop back i am not familiar with it yes i have opened the ports required per the rustdesk documentation are the clients that are failing to connect on the same network as your server if so look in to nat loop back you also must make sure you open the correct ports on your firewall router where your rustdesk server is connected

google verify always uses the first number are you aware of how it works you access your financial data it pops a message on your phone asking if you are doing it when you choose yes it then says to select the number of 3 it is supposed to be random which number it chosen could be 1 2 or 3 in my case it is always the first number see the pic i attached i just generated this number it gave me was 9 it always is whatever is first on this list this is a great find should definitely be reported and checked to be fixed if it s always the first number shown it renders that specific 2fa to be ineffective at least the one confirming from a choice of 3 numbers on other devices what behaviour are you expecting exactly the first number is the default 2fa number the others are simply backups
